_publications._publicationTitle: SVA retrotransposon insertion-associated deletion represents a novel mutational mechanism underlying large genomic copy number changes with non-recurrent breakpoints.

_contextSnippet: Breakpoint-spanning PCRs, using the Expand Long Template PCR system under standard conditions with primers designed on the basis of the array CGH results, were successful in the case of 15 of the 17 atypical NF1 deletions
details: Successful in 15 of 17 atypical NF1 deletions (88% success rate) for breakpoint-spanning PCRs under standard conditions
